common_execution.decision: All price features use only prior completed Chicago trading sessions; calendar membership is known from the frozen CME session calendar.
common_execution.overlap: One event per candidate per Chicago trading day.
common_execution.roll: Prior observations and current execution must each remain explicit-contract correct; no return is computed across a contract change.
common_execution.costs: Product/session V7 round-turn cost at multipliers 1.0, 1.5 and 2.0.

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: Regular pension, payroll and index-allocation cash flows create persistent buy demand around the last and first three eligible trading sessions of each month.
hypotheses.who_pays: Benchmark-constrained allocators executing predictable calendar cash flows rather than optimizing intraday price.
hypotheses.activation: Last eligible Chicago trading session of a calendar month or one of the first three eligible sessions of the next month, determined from the frozen exchange calendar.

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: A large prior cash-session move reflects information and institutional parent orders that are only partially incorporated before the close and continue during the next cash session.
hypotheses.who_pays: Slow institutional execution and participants anchoring to the prior close despite unfinished information incorporation.
hypotheses.activation_threshold: median absolute prior-session return over the immediately preceding 60 eligible sessions; minimum 20 histories

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: An extreme cumulative two-session move exhausts short-horizon liquidity demand and reverses when temporary inventory holders unwind on the third session.
hypotheses.who_pays: Late trend followers and liquidity takers entering after a two-day inventory shock is substantially complete.
hypotheses.feature: sum of the two immediately preceding explicit-contract RTH log returns
hypotheses.activation_threshold: 75th percentile of absolute two-session returns over the immediately preceding 60 eligible observations; minimum 30 histories

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: An extreme crude-oil repricing changes inflation expectations before gold allocators finish adjusting, leaving a same-direction next-session GC response.
hypotheses.who_pays: Cross-asset allocators updating inflation hedges after rather than during the initial energy shock.
hypotheses.activation_threshold: 75th percentile of absolute source return over the immediately preceding 60 common eligible sessions

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: An extreme equity repricing changes expected growth demand before physical-energy hedgers fully adjust, leaving a same-direction next-session CL response.
hypotheses.who_pays: Energy participants updating macro demand expectations after the initial equity-index shock.
hypotheses.activation_threshold: 75th percentile of absolute source return over the immediately preceding 60 common eligible sessions

hypotheses.economic_hypothesis: An extreme equity repricing triggers safe-haven reallocation that reaches gold with a one-session lag, producing an opposite-direction next-session GC response.
hypotheses.who_pays: Portfolio allocators sequencing equity de-risking before safe-haven completion.
hypotheses.activation_threshold: 75th percentile of absolute source return over the immediately preceding 60 common eligible sessions

CONTRE: Calendar and lagged daily mechanisms have high event density but can reduce to generic long bias or autocorrelation; matched non-signal days, sign inversion, DSR/BH and the permanent null tripwire must distinguish that before any fresh proof.
